name: Float Resource Management API
what_it_does: Resource planning and team scheduling platform with REST API — managing people, projects, and scheduled allocations for service and consulting firms, with capacity view, time-off management, and budget tracking via API.
use_cases: Querying team capacity and utilization for project planning automation
Creating and updating project allocations programmatically from project management systems
Syncing Float resource data with CRM/ERP for project-based billing
Monitoring team availability and forecasting capacity gaps via reporting API
Building custom dashboards on Float data using the REST API
not_for: Simple time tracking without planning (use Clockify or Toggl)
Shift scheduling for hourly workers (use Deputy or 7shifts)
Enterprise ERP resource management with complex financial modeling
best_when: A professional services, agency, or consulting firm needs visual resource planning with API access for CRM/PM integration and automated capacity reporting.
avoid_when: You need time tracking (Float is for scheduling future work, not logging past time), or hourly shift scheduling.

auth.notes: API key from account settings. Authorization header with Bearer token. Account-scoped access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Admin permissions recommended for full data access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $6/person/month; Pro from $10/person/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: No free tier. Person-based pricing. Australian company. API available on all paid plans. Strong agency and consulting firm adoption. Good documentation. 5,000+ companies use Float for resource planning.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covering people, projects, allocations, tasks, and time-off. Good documentation. No webhooks — must poll for data changes. API key auth is simple. Allocation data is the core resource planning entity. Useful for capacity reporting automation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Allocation creation creates new allocations — not idempotent. People and project management idempotent by identifier. Read operations always idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: No webhooks — must poll for schedule changes; polling adds latency to event-driven workflows
Allocations are future-planned, not past time tracking — different mental model
Bulk allocation creation not supported — must create allocations individually
Person/project IDs required — must fetch and cache IDs before creating allocations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Australian/US data residency. API key auth. No OAuth2 scopes. Standard SaaS security practices. No formal certifications beyond GDPR. Small team with good security culture.
